parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-631 (Risk B, 40 units, RP20.P06.M02.S19-RP20.P06.M04.S14 — the P06 permission/audit type-shape test tail plus primary/secondary permission-matrix bridge descriptor pack). I traced bindings rather than trusting evidence claims: the CP631 registry binding (pack_id CP00-631, upstream CP00-630, next CP00-632 / RP20.P06.M04.S15, production_ready_flag data_room_vdr_core_p06_type_shape_tail_primary_secondary_bridge_descriptor_verified) matches the contract projections (cp631_pack_binding/requirements/no_write_attestation and the full cp631 descriptor projection), the program head (current_pack_id CP00-631, next_pack_id CP00-632, runtime_opened false, descriptor_only true), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the review baseline, and next-pack-queue.json (head CP00-632). I recomputed coverage from the row arrays (P06_PERMISSION_AUDIT_TEST_TAIL_ROWS 4 for M02, P06_PERMISSION_AUDIT_EXTENDED_ROWS = P06_PERMISSION_AUDIT_ROWS 20 + Cross-tenant/Leak-prevention 2 = 22 for M03, P06_PERMISSION_AUDIT_HEAD_14_ROWS = slice(0,14) = 14 for M04) = 40 across 3 sections, and independently re-derived the deliverable distribution via dataRoomVdrCoreDeliverableType (test 8, security_audit 7, implementation 16, ui 7, claude_review 2) — matching the registry DELIVERABLE_COUNTS, the validator's enforced expected_deliverable_counts, the manifest, and the prompt's deliverable_distribution and micro_title_distribution (Type And Shape Definition 4, Primary Implementation Slice 22, Secondary Workflow Slice 14) exactly. The freezeCp631Result->freezeCp630Result->...->freezeCp610Result chain forces every dispatch/runtime/write/leak flag false and synthetic_only/no_real_data true; the descriptor factory ignores input (void input) so no real deal data can enter; validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp631P06TypeShapeTailPrimarySecondaryBridgeDescriptor asserts the full no-write set including evaluates_runtime_permission false and writes_permission_decision false (critical because this pack covers the permission-matrix/decision-binding rows) plus the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true) and returns only production_ready_candidate:true — never an enterprise-trust claim. Hermes is used solely as a deterministic descriptor packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; program_contract runtime_opened false, descriptor_only true); Claude is encoded as a read-only, non-final-approval reviewer with allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob and forbidden_review_evidence covering the CP178+ blockers (not_logged_in, zero_byte_output, malformed_json, fenced_json_only, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, enterprise_trust_claim). Three dedicated CP631 tests deep-equal the contract projections to package code and assert the CP00-632 / RP20.P06.M04.S15 handoff; index.js re-exports the API via export *; all nine required_public_exports are present. No premature claim exists anywhere: manifest status in_progress / production_ready false / runtime_ready false, command-evidence production_ready false, construction-inspection PENDING, adjudication pending, claude-review-result review_pending with empty valid_review_receipts. CP178+ hardening checks hold: the readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON result), validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present false, prompt within size, and no full diff/source embedded, and there is no stale canonical receipt (this is the first review attempt; no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json/invalid-attempts exist yet). I did not infer the unrelated dirty files (workbook/*, ldip, progress-control-room, the .docx, closeout-pack-plan.json, next-pack-queue.json, new-session-handoff.md) as part of this pack per the changed-file-scope boundary. No P0/P1/P2 defects found. Findings are limited to P3 governance/traceability preconditions; production_ready must remain false until governance normalization and human final approval, and this verdict does not weaken that gate.

findings.title: Closeout governance artifacts remain PENDING and must be normalized with this review receipt
findings.details: construction-inspection.json (verdict PENDING, production_ready false, pack_production_ready false, pack_level_claude_review_completed false, claude_findings p0-p3 all pending), adjudication.md (P0-P3 findings all pending, Production ready after adjudication pending), and claude-review-result.json (status review_pending, exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run false, valid_review_receipts empty, parsed_review.overall_verdict PENDING with blocks_pack_closeout true) are all in the expected pre-refresh state for an in_progress pack whose Claude review (this run) has not yet been normalized. They must be updated to reflect this review's normalized receipt, and production_ready must stay false until human final approval per the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approver false, human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim true).
findings.recommendation: Normalize this review's receipt into claude-review-result.json (rejecting any auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped/missing-field evidence and any claude_final_approval/enterprise_trust claim), then complete adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json; keep production_ready gated on human approval and do not weaken the gate.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pipeline step after a passing review; no change required to the delivered descriptor code. The descriptor, validators, contract projections, and tests are already consistent.

findings.title: Command-matrix outcomes are recorded evidence and cannot be re-executed under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records the full expected validation matrix (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, plus node --check x5, rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate, node --test model.test.js, and build) all at exit_code 0, with status passed but production_ready false and gate_outcome passed_after_pack_evidence_creation_pending_review_refresh. As a read-only reviewer I confirm the matrix is present and consistent with the baseline's expected commands, and that the source it attests to (descriptor/validator/contract/test consistency) holds, but I cannot independently re-execute the commands.
findings.recommendation: Before the human-approved production_ready flip, confirm the recorded matrix was executed against the current tree (notably git diff --check clean) and remains green; keep production_ready false until then.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; consistent with the pack's stated gate_outcome and the read-only review model.
